title: Fixes to WebQSP Indexer and GRetriever so that unnecessary disk writes are reduced.
body: Quick fix to webqsp and gretriever to help with unnecessary cruft on disk and out of date api in examples.

step_plan.summary: Reduce unnecessary disk writes in the WebQSP/CWQ dataset pipeline by making raw/processed artifacts and the LargeGraphIndexer cache more idempotent, and update the GRetriever example to avoid unconditional saves and align with the current model API.

steps.operation: Make raw dataset download idempotent by skipping HuggingFace `save_to_disk(...)` when raw cache already exists (unless `force_reload` is requested), and avoid rewriting the same raw artifact directory.
steps.reason: `download()` currently materializes the HF dataset on disk, which can create redundant artifacts and overwrite an existing raw cache even when unchanged; guarding this reduces unnecessary disk churn.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior for users relying on implicit re-download/refresh; ensure `force_reload` (or equivalent) still triggers a rebuild and that partial/corrupt raw dirs are handled (e.g., by detecting incomplete cache and re-downloading).

steps.operation: Tighten the process decision logic to reuse existing processed artifacts (indexer + split files) and only trigger `_build_graph()`/`_retrieve_subgraphs()` when outputs are missing or `force_reload` is enabled.
steps.reason: `process()` is the central orchestration point; it should short-circuit when processed paths exist to prevent repeated rebuilding and subsequent writes on every dataset instantiation.
steps.side_effects: Incorrect existence checks could lead to stale outputs being reused; add robust checks (e.g., verify required files for a split) and ensure behavior remains correct when users request a different split/config that changes the processed filename set.

steps.operation: Avoid rebuilding/resaving the large graph indexer when an on-disk indexer already exists and is compatible; load via `LargeGraphIndexer.from_disk(...)` and only call `save(...)` when newly built or explicitly overwriting is requested.
steps.reason: `_build_graph()` triggers expensive indexer construction and calls `indexer.save(...)`; skipping rebuild/resave when cache exists directly reduces the main source of unnecessary writes.
steps.side_effects: Requires that `from_disk()` can reliably determine compatibility; if embeddings/model config changes, you may need a version/hash check to force rebuild, which can affect cache invalidation semantics and reproducibility.

steps.operation: Make subgraph retrieval caching-aware by skipping `self.save(results_graphs, path)` when the processed split file(s) already exist (unless `force_reload`), and avoid generating/saving splits that are not requested.
steps.reason: `_retrieve_subgraphs()` writes processed per-split artifacts; preventing re-saving identical outputs and limiting to the requested split reduces disk cruft and speeds repeated runs.
steps.side_effects: If upstream retrieval parameters change without changing the output path, stale files could be reused; consider embedding key configuration into filenames or storing minimal metadata for invalidation.

steps.operation: Refactor indexer persistence to be non-destructive by default: add a safe save strategy (e.g., `overwrite=False` default, early return if target exists, or atomic write to temp + rename) instead of unconditional `shutil.rmtree(...)` and full rewrite.
steps.reason: Current `save()` deletes and rewrites the entire directory, producing unnecessary I/O and churn even when nothing changed; making saves idempotent/atomic directly addresses the PR goal.
steps.side_effects: Changing defaults can affect callers that expect a fresh rewrite; ensure callers can still request overwrite explicitly. Atomic rename may behave differently on some filesystems; add cleanup of temp dirs on failure.

steps.operation: Keep disk loading compatible with the adjusted save behavior by handling both existing layout and any new atomic/temp strategy; add validation/error messaging when the indexer directory is incomplete or mid-write.
steps.reason: If `save()` becomes non-destructive/atomic, `from_disk()` must tolerate partially written states (e.g., temp directories) and provide robust checks so datasets can safely reuse cached indexers without surprises.
steps.side_effects: Stricter validation could surface errors that previously went unnoticed (but would later cause subtle runtime issues). Ensure any new compatibility logic does not silently load wrong/stale artifacts.

steps.operation: Gate all end-of-run writes behind a user option (e.g., `--save_checkpoint`, `--output_dir`, or `--no_save`): only call `save_params_dict(...)` and `torch.save(...)` when explicitly enabled, and default to not writing to CWD.
steps.reason: The example currently saves model params and evaluation outputs unconditionally, producing unnecessary disk artifacts during routine runs; making saving opt-in aligns with “reduce unnecessary disk writes”.
steps.side_effects: Example behavior changes (no artifacts by default). Update documentation/CLI help accordingly. Downstream users relying on implicit saved files will need to pass the new flag.

steps.operation: Update the saving helper to support the new save policy: accept an output directory/path, create directories as needed, and no-op cleanly when saving is disabled (or when path is None).
steps.reason: Centralizing the “optional save” behavior in `save_params_dict` reduces duplication and ensures consistent, minimal disk writes across checkpoints/final saves.
steps.side_effects: Call sites must be updated to pass the new arguments; ensure backward compatibility if the helper is used elsewhere in the examples. If directory creation is added, it may affect relative path assumptions.

steps.operation: Modernize the training loss computation to match the current `GRetriever`/LLM forward API (argument names/return shape), ensuring the example does not rely on deprecated signatures.
steps.reason: The PR mentions “out of date api in examples”; `get_loss` is the key adapter between the example loop and model APIs, so it should be updated to the current expected call contract.
steps.side_effects: May require adjusting batch structure assumptions and metrics logging. A change here can break the example at runtime if not aligned with the actual model output types; validate by running the example after updates.

steps.operation: Modernize the inference path to match the current inference/generation API (e.g., `GRetriever.inference(...)` or model callable signature), and ensure it does not trigger any implicit saving/logging to disk.
steps.reason: `inference_step` is the other main API touchpoint; updating it prevents runtime errors due to signature drift and helps keep the example free of unintended side effects.
steps.side_effects: May change the structure of returned predictions/eval outputs; ensure downstream evaluation code in the example still works and that optional saving in `train` serializes the correct object when enabled.
